findings.summary: The new malformed-eventTimestamp HTTP test (and 2 of its 3 sibling validation-rejection tests) only assert the error code, not that message is a meaningful, non-empty explanation.
findings.detail: A passing test for this checklist item should assert body.Message is non-null/non-empty (as PostEvents_InvalidPayload_Returns400WithErrorShape at line 70 already does for the amount<=0 case) for every one of the 4 validation-rejection HTTP tests, not just one of them -- otherwise a regression that returned message: "" or message: null alongside the correct error code would pass undetected. This is a pre-existing pattern in 2 of the 3 tests this diff's new test mirrors (PostEvents_MissingRequiredField_Returns400WithErrorShape line 84, PostEvents_InvalidType_Returns400WithErrorShape line 97), not introduced by this diff, but the new malformed-eventTimestamp test (line 109) was an opportunity to close it and instead perpetuated the weaker pattern.

disposition.reasoning: Added Assert.NotNull(body.Message) to the 3 tests missing it (missing-field, invalid-type, malformed-eventTimestamp). Full suite (94 tests) passes.
